bids.reasoning: At the opening with no opponent offers or messages yet, I am targeting my maximum utility bid. Since there is no time pressure at t=0.0, I should start firm on my preferred values across all issues and wait for the opponent’s response.

At t=0.02 it is still very early, so I am targeting a very high-utility bid while making only a small concession from my opening position. The opponent’s offer was all valueA, which is very low utility for me, and their message is an emotional appeal without evidence of real concession, so I will concede only on a low-weight issue for now.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still very early in the negotiation and the opponent has not shown any real concession, repeating the all-valueA offer twice. Their messages are emotional appeals, but the offer history is the reliable signal, so I am only making a small concession on a low-weight issue while holding firm on the more important issues C, D, and E.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid around 0.96 because it is still very early, and the opponent’s offers remain far below my preferred outcomes despite a small move on issueE. Their messages emphasize cooperation and need, but the actual bidding behavior shows only minimal concession, so I will concede slightly on my lower-weight issues while holding firm on issueC, issueD, and issueE.

I am targeting a high utility around 0.94 because it is still very early, so there is no need for major concessions yet. The opponent has only moved slightly, improving issueE from valueA to valueB, while their messages are emotional appeals rather than binding evidence. I will concede further on a low-weight issueA while holding firm on the high-weight issues C, D, and E.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.92 because it is still very early at t=0.10 and the opponent’s offers remain extremely unfavorable to me, with only small concessions so far. Their messages emphasize cooperation and need, but the actual bids show they are mostly holding near valueA, so I will concede only on lower-weight issues while protecting issueC, issueD, and issueE.

I am targeting about 0.90 utility because it is still early (t=0.12) and the opponent’s offers remain very low for me despite small concessions. Their latest move improved issueE to valueC, but since issueE and issueD are my highest-weight issues, I will hold those at valueE and concede further on a low-weight issue instead. The opponent’s emotional appeals are noted, but the actual bid history shows only limited movement.

I am targeting a still-high utility around 0.88 because it is early at t=0.14 and the opponent’s offers remain very low for me, though they have made small concessions on issueD/issueE. Their messages are emotional appeals, but the actual bids show only limited movement, so I will concede mainly on my low-weight issueA while holding firm on the high-weight issues C/D/E.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.86 because it is still early, but I will continue conceding on my low-weight issues first. The opponent’s offers have improved only slightly for me, with movement mostly on issueD/issueE while issueA-C remain very low, and their emotional messages do not change the numeric assessment. To avoid repeating my last bid, I am conceding further on issueB while holding firm on the high-weight issues issueC, issueD, and issueE.

I am targeting a strong utility around 0.84, since it is still early at t=0.18 and there is no need to make large concessions yet. The opponent’s offers have improved somewhat for me, especially their latest move to issueE=valueE, but they are still holding issueA/issueB/issueC/issueD at low values; their messages are emotional appeals, so I rely mainly on the bid history. I will concede further on the low-weight issues A and B while holding firm on the higher-weight issues C, D, and E.

I am targeting about 0.80 utility by preserving my highest-weight issues D and E at valueE while making a moderate concession on issueC and having already conceded the low-weight issues A and B. At t=0.20 there is still time, so I should not drop to the opponent’s low offers yet; their messages are emotional appeals, but the actual bids show only limited concession and remain low-value for me.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.76, since it is early enough that I should not drop close to the opponent’s best offers yet. The opponent has made only limited concessions, with their best offers to me around 0.44, and their messages are mostly emotional appeals rather than reliable constraints. I will continue conceding on lower-weight issues and now make a modest concession on issueC while holding firm on the high-weight issueD and issueE.

I targeted a still-strong utility around 0.72, conceding further on issueC while preserving issueD and issueE, which are my highest-weight issues. At t=0.24 there is no need to collapse to the opponent’s low-utility offers, especially since their bids remain mostly near valueA and their messages are emotional appeals rather than reliable constraints.

I am targeting about 0.68 utility: a meaningful concession from my last bid by giving ground on lower/mid-weight issueC while still protecting the high-weight issueD and issueE. At t=0.26 there is still time, so I should not collapse to the opponent’s low offers yet; their messages are emotional appeals, while their actual bids remain much worse for me and even their latest offer is below their earlier best.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.62 by holding firm on one of my highest-weight issues while conceding further on the other high-weight issue. At t=0.28 there is some time pressure but it is not yet late, and the opponent’s offers remain much lower for me despite occasional movement, especially their latest willingness to put issueE at valueD. Their messages are emotional appeals, so I’m relying on the bid pattern rather than the claims.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.56: still well above reservation, but with meaningful concessions given we are at t=0.30. The opponent’s messages are emotional appeals, but their offers remain low for me and have not steadily improved, so I am conceding mainly on lower-weight issues and one step on issueD while keeping issueE at a strong value.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.54, conceding slightly from my previous bid while still protecting the high-weight issues D and E. At t=0.32 it is still early-mid negotiation, so I do not need to accept the opponent’s low offers, but their recent movement on issueC and issueD suggests a useful compromise path. Their emotional messages are noted as cheap talk; the actual bids show they are still holding issueA/issueB very low and only selectively improving higher-weight issues.

I am targeting about 0.56 utility: I keep issueE at valueE because it is highly important to me and the opponent has repeatedly signaled willingness there, while conceding fully on low-weight issues A/B/C and partially on issueD. At t=0.34 there is still time, so I should not collapse to the opponent’s bid, but their recent offers suggest issueE can be traded against lower values elsewhere; their emotional messages do not add binding information beyond the bid history.
